rulings.Case Title: GUARDIANSHIP OF RODOLFO LEVIT CHAVEZ
rulings.Calendar Matter: ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR Status On Filing Receipt For Distribution
rulings.Rulings: Hearing. Appearance required. Issues may include (but not limited to): Guardian has not filed receipt for final distribution to former minor. PARTIES APPEARING SHOULD CHECK IN FOR THE HEARING WITH THEIR FIRST INITIAL AND LAST NAME. ***Until further notice, the Court strongly encourages remote appearances by video or telephone. Counsel and parties may appear by video or audio-only telephone through Zoom. For a video appearance, go to zoom.us, click "join a meeting" and input meeting ID 875 8017 7925 and password 722809. For an audio-only appearance through Zoom, call 1-669-900-6833 and key in meeting ID "875 8017 7925#", then participant ID "0#", then password "722809#". (Toll rates may apply).) Counsel and parties may appear by audio-only telephone through CourtCall. Review the Court's 6th Amended General Order which took effect on 07/21/2020 for more information. If counsel or any party appears in person, he or she should anticipate delays at the entrance to the courthouse and inside the courthouse. Masks must be worn at all times while in the courthouse and social distancing protocols will be strictly enforced, including inside the courtroom. Anyone making an in-person appearance must first familiarize themselves with the Court's current pandemic-related requirements and protocols by reviewing the Court's website at https://sfsuperiorcourt.org/.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF PETER MON YU HUNG
rulings.Calendar Matter: ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: REPORT, FEES, FINAL DISTRIBUTION
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 11/24/2020 (no earlier date available). The following issues must be addressed: 1) Has the petitioner filed and served notice of rejection of Tri Union Frozen Product's creditor claim? No DE-174 form is on file. Probate Code section 9250(a) & CRC 7.401. 2) Paragraph 20 alleges that the estate cash has been kept in interest-bearing accounts, but paragraph 23 does not show any cash as the estate's assets on hand. 3) Kwai Jone Hung's disclaimer was filed with the Court more than 9 months after the decedent's death. Was the disclaimer timely? 4) If the estate has no cash on hand, has the beneficiary advanced funds to the estate for payment of the statutory compensation and reserve? Probate Code section 10831(d). 5) The addresses of Alexander Merchant and Glassberg, Pollak & Associates listed in the proof of service are incomplete.

rulings.Case Title: CONSERVATORSHIP OF EVA SMALL
rulings.Calendar Matter: ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Accounting #1, Report, Fees
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 11/24/2020 (no earlier date available). 1) The proof of service does not show notice was given to the conservatee. 2) Paragraph 9 of the petition needs to address CRC 7.703(e)(5). 3) Does the conservatee own the Toyota? Has it been inventoried? 4) Schedule A appears to include social security benefits payable to the conservatee's son. 5) Were the purchases of "new water heater" and "new refrigerator" reported on Schedule C authorized by the Court? 6) Please itemize the legal costs paid on 11/18/2019. 7) Please provide a schedule of proposed distributions to the general conservator. 8) As to Exhibit A: A) How many hours were spent on internal communications (including internal memoranda) between the paralegal and attorney on 07/09/2019, 07/29/2019, 08/14/2019, 08/16/2019, 11/08/2019, 11/13/2019, 12/09/2019, 01/22/2020, 01/21/2020, 01/24/2020, 01/31/2020, 02/10/2020, 02/12/2012, 02/26/2020, 03/18/2020, 03/23/2020 and 04/06/2020? B) Should the paralegal/attorney's time to obtain the ex parte nunc pro tunc order correcting the errors be charged to the estate? C) Should the paralegal's court appearance time on 08/29/2019 be charged to the estate in addition to the attorney's? D) Should the attorney's time to prepare "retainer agreement" on 07/01/2019 charged? E) Did the paralegal and attorney duplicate any work (in particular for client communications) and double-bill on 08/16/2019, 09/04/2019, 11/11/2019, 11/13/2019, 11/18/2019, 01/24/2020, and 03/23/2020? F) Paragraph 8 of the petition alleges that the paralegal/attorney's services were rendered to the petitioner as temporary conservator, but most services after 01/23/2020 described in Exhibit A appear to have been rendered to the general conservator as a new client.

rulings.Case Title: IN THE MATTER OF THE MARITAL TRUST
rulings.Calendar Matter: Petition Confirming Appointment Of Successor Trustees
rulings.Rulings: Hearing. Appearance required. Court is to determine whether Article Eighth of governing trust should be interpreted to authorize "Trustee" other than James Crafts, Jr. and John Berl to nominate and appoint successor co-trustee. PARTIES APPEARING SHOULD CHECK IN FOR THE HEARING WITH THEIR FIRST INITIAL AND LAST NAME. ***Until further notice, the Court strongly encourages remote appearances by video or telephone. Counsel and parties may appear by video or audio-only telephone through Zoom. For a video appearance, go to zoom.us, click "join a meeting" and input meeting ID 875 8017 7925 and password 722809. For an audio-only appearance through Zoom, call 1-669-900-6833 and key in meeting ID "875 8017 7925#", then participant ID "0#", then password "722809#". (Toll rates may apply).) Counsel and parties may appear by audio-only telephone through CourtCall. Review the Court's 6th Amended General Order which took effect on 07/21/2020 for more information. If counsel or any party appears in person, he or she should anticipate delays at the entrance to the courthouse and inside the courthouse. Masks must be worn at all times while in the courthouse and social distancing protocols will be strictly enforced, including inside the courtroom. Anyone making an in-person appearance must first familiarize themselves with the Court's current pandemic-related requirements and protocols by reviewing the Court's website at https://sfsuperiorcourt.org/.

rulings.Case Title: IN THE MATTER OF THE DIANE D. LUCAS REVOCABLE TRUST
rulings.Calendar Matter: Petition For Order Determining Trust'S Title To Personal Property
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 12/9/20. If the issues below cannot be addressed in time to give 30 days' notice after the supplement or amendment is filed, as the case may be (along with a copy of the supplement or amendment), the hearing will need to be continued further. The incorrect (outdated) form of Notice was used to serve notice. All petitions involving a trust must state the names and last known addresses of all vested or contingent beneficiaries. [CRC 7.902.] Unless an exception applies, such beneficiaries must either receive or waive notice. Copies of the bank statements immediately preceding the settlor's death must be filed to show how title to the accounts was held. (This Court will only confirm specific assets to a trust that are supported by evidence, not by an "omnibus" order.) Prob. C. Sect. 850 succeeded Heggstad and sets higher standards for proving that assets not transferred to a trust during a trustor's life were, in fact, intended to be held in the trust. A beneficiary of the trust receives mailed notice with a copy of the petition, but any heir-at-law, who would stand to inherit, if the trust did not exist or were invalid, is a party whose right, title or interest would be affected by this petition, and, therefore, must receive (or waive) personal service of notice. [Prob. C. Sect. 17203(b) and CCP Sect. 413.10 et seq.] Are all heirs-at-law trust beneficiaries?

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF MICHAEL FRANCIS MCGOVERN
rulings.Calendar Matter: PETITION FOR PROBATE OF WILL AND FOR LETTERS TESTAMENTARY
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 11/18/20. An amended petition was just filed on 10/20/20, which was not at least 15 days before this hearing. An amending pleading requires the same notice as the pleading it amends, including publication. [CRC 7.53(a).] No courtesy copies have been received by the Examiner as of 10/22/20. Courtesy copies of the amended petition and all papers filed thereafter, including proof of service of notice of the amended petition and proof of publication of notice of the amended petition, must be received by the Examiner by no later than 11/10/20.
